question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition window between the EntityService.getEncodedId() call and the TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances() call by flooding concurrent requests, causing the controller to return a partially-resolved ownerAccountId against a now-unreachable DB, leading to a hung or inconsistent response?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with a valid idOrAliasOrEvmAddress that forces EntityService.getEncodedId() to issue a DB lookup, then if the DB partitions mid-lookup, cause the promise to hang indefinitely with no timeout enforced at the controller layer, exhausting the Node.js event loop?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of requests to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/tokens during a DB network partition, causing the connection pool to be fully exhausted by pending getAccountTokenAllowances() promises, resulting in a denial-of-service for all other API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply spender.id and token.id bounds that force extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() to produce a three-part UNION ALL query (lower/inner/upper), and if the DB partitions after the first sub-query executes but before the second, cause the controller to return a partial result set without any error, violating data completeness guarantees?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a circuit-breaker in getAccountTokenAllowances() so that during a DB network partition, every in-flight request blocks on await TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances(query), accumulating unbounded memory for pending promise chains until the process OOMs?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests using an EVM address as idOrAliasOrEvmAddress, forcing EntityService.getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() to issue a DB query, and if the DB partitions during that query, cause the error thrown to propagate as an unhandled rejection that crashes the process rather than returning a structured error response?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests using an account alias as idOrAliasOrEvmAddress, forcing EntityService.getAccountIdFromAlias() to issue a DB query, and if the DB partitions mid-query, cause the controller to return a 500 error that leaks internal DB connection error details (host, port, pool state) in the response body?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply the maximum allowed limit value so that during a DB network partition, the UNION ALL query holds a DB connection open for the maximum statement timeout duration, amplifying the partition impact by preventing connection pool recycling?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the two-phase DB access pattern (EntityService.getEncodedId() then TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances()) so that a transient network partition between the two calls causes the second call to use a stale or recycled connection that returns data from a different DB replica, producing inconsistent allowance results?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated requests during a DB partition recovery window, causing the connection pool to oscillate between failed and recovered states, resulting in non-deterministic responses (some 200 OK with data, some 500) for identical requests, breaking client-side idempotency assumptions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with both spender.id gte/lte bounds and token.id gte/lte bounds to force a three-sub-query UNION ALL, and during a DB partition, cause only the lower sub-query to complete before the connection drops, returning a truncated result set with a valid HTTP 200 status and a next pagination link pointing to data that no longer exists?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getPaginationLink()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a DB partition that causes TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances() to return an empty rows array (due to connection reset), causing getPaginationLink() to compute isEnd=true and return null for the next link, permanently hiding paginated data that exists in the DB once the partition heals?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send a burst of requests with different idOrAliasOrEvmAddress values during a DB partition, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to queue multiple concurrent DB lookups that all time out simultaneously, producing a thundering-herd reconnection storm when the partition heals?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of request-level timeout enforcement in getAccountTokenAllowances() so that during a prolonged DB partition, the HTTP response is never sent, causing the client TCP connection to remain open and consuming a file descriptor on the server until the OS limit is reached?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply order=desc with spender.id and token.id bounds to force a specific UNION ALL structure, and during a DB partition, cause the orderClause to be applied only to the completed sub-queries, returning results in an incorrect order that violates the API contract and misleads downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the async/await chain in getAccountTokenAllowances() so that a DB partition causes an unhandled promise rejection (if the error is not caught by the framework's error handler), leading to a Node.js process crash that takes down the entire REST API service?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a valid numeric account ID (bypassing the DB lookup in getEncodedId()) so that EntityService.getEncodedId() returns immediately but TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances() blocks on a partitioned DB, creating an asymmetric load pattern that exhausts the DB connection pool faster than the alias/EVM path?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id=eq filter (single equality) so that extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() routes all filters into the lower sub-query, and during a DB partition, the single sub-query hangs indefinitely, blocking the response while consuming a DB connection for the full statement timeout duration?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a DB partition that causes TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances() to throw a DbError, and if the error handler serializes the error object including the sqlQuery and params fields, cause the full parameterized SQL query (including ownerAccountId) to be leaked in the HTTP error response?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user time requests to coincide with a DB failover (primary→replica promotion), causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to resolve against the old primary and TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances() to query the new primary before replication sync, returning allowance data that is inconsistent with the resolved account ID?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply the maximum limit value (e.g., 100) combined with spender.id and token.id range bounds to maximize the UNION ALL query complexity, so that during a DB partition, the query holds three separate DB connections open simultaneously, tripling the connection pool drain rate compared to a simple query?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getAccountTokenAllowances() does not implement retry logic, so that a transient DB partition (lasting less than 1 second) causes a permanent 500 error for that request, while a retry by the client would succeed, creating a false impression of data unavailability?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getPaginationLink()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a DB partition that causes a partial result (fewer rows than limit) to be returned, causing getPaginationLink() to set isEnd=true and return null for the next link, even though the DB has more rows, permanently truncating the visible allowance set for that account until the client re-queries from the beginning?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an EVM address that requires a DB lookup in getEntityIdFromEvmAddress(), and during a DB partition, cause the entityFromEvmAddressQuery to return 0 rows (connection reset before rows arrive), triggering a NotFoundError that permanently marks a valid account as not found in any caching layer?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply conflicting bounds (e.g., spender.id=gte:1&spender.id=lte:1000 with token.id=gte:1&token.id=lte:1000) to force the maximum three-part UNION ALL, and during a DB partition, cause the inner sub-query to execute on a different DB connection than the lower sub-query, returning results from different transaction snapshots and violating read consistency?
